title: Ditassa franciscoi
taxonomy: Ditassa franciscoi is classified under the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane family. This family comprises over 5,000 species of flowering plants, many of which are native to tropical and subtropical regions. The genus Ditassa is further classified under the subfamily Asclepiadoideae, which includes about 700 species of milkweed and related plants. The species Ditassa franciscoi is likely a member of this diverse group, although more specific information may be required to confirm its classification. Further research may be needed to determine the exact classification of Ditassa franciscoi.

intro: Ditassa franciscoi is a plant species belonging to the Apocynaceae family, commonly known as a climbing vine native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Ditassa franciscoi include alkaloids, terpenoids, and flavonoids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Ditassa franciscoi is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, as well as being incorporated into various folk remedies and tonics.
